medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with concern for obstruction due to lack of ostomy output for 3 days with emesis/constipation. Output improved with magnesium citrate and milk of magnesia. Underwent bronchoscopy for tumor-related obstruction of bronchus intermedius.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Metastatic poorly differentiated sarcoma involving the rectum, prostate, bladder, and lungs. History of severe constipation, hydronephrosis, UTI, and diverting colostomy.
medical_history.family_history: Brother is diabetic and has CHD, son has testicular cancer.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: 98.4, 67-84, 101-128/53-67, 93-96% on RA at rest and 95% with ambulation. Alert and oriented. No acute distress.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: A&O x3, gait WNL. Cardiovascular: RRR, no MRG. Abdomen: Soft, nontender, no mass, no organomegaly. The diverting colostomy has a pink well hydrated mucosa with green-brown stool.

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: WBC increased to 14.0*; RBC decreased to 3.29*; Hgb decreased to 9.4*; Hct decreased to 28.0*

laboratory_examinations.findings: UreaN-22*, Creat-1.7*, Na-140, K-3.9, Cl-101, HCO3-27, AnGap-16
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: UreaN decreased to 14; Creat remained elevated at 1.7; Na decreased to 137; K remained stable at 3.9

radiographic_examinations.findings: No abnormally dilated loops of bowel.

Post bronchoscopy call PICC opacities, likely reflecting a combination of atelectasis and local bleeding at the right lung bases. No abnormalities at the left lung bases. Mild cardiomegaly with elongation of the descending aorta.
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Compared to the previous radiograph, findings are consistent with post-bronchoscopy changes.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Demonstrated a 5-cm soft tissue lesion in the rectal vault with increased density in surrounding perirectal fat.
Bulky rectal mass measuring 9.8 cm, extending from the anorectal junction to the upper rectum, compatible with a T4 tumor.
Showed metastatic disease in the right lower lobe and subpleural pulmonary lesions as well as lymph nodes.

other_tests.findings: 100% occluded bronchus intermedius with tumor; tumor destruction and debridement achieved.
Demonstrated a very poorly differentiated malignant neoplasm with chondroid differentiation.
